author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
<@1000001410538151996> 

Keep in mind I am  ONLY talking about post-Gloas.

1)  "A node can always eager push" -> sure, but only it's own parts metadata. We're removing the header in Gloas and also -> we're removing the code path where a block proposer eager pushes it's cells because we agreed that in Gloas, it made no sense to do so as you need the block to verify the cells anyways (even the data columns no longer have the KZG commitments). 

In fact, we're adding code in Prysm where we will Downscore peers who send us cells before we've sent them our parts metadata after looking up our EL. See https://github.com/OffchainLabs/prysm/pull/16873. 

2) "is also not correct. For example, Lighthouse propagates any columns it receives eagerly before getBlobs returns"

You can propogate columns but you can only do so after you have the KZG commitments from the block to verify that column. So you can't/wont propogate the columns unless you've seen the block for it because otherwise you are propogating unveirified columns.

Basically, what I sam saying is that given how partial cells work post Gloas, (I send you my parts metadata after looking up the EL -> only then do you send me the cells I am missing -> we've removed eager pushing of header/cells in Gloas -> so I should only ever get cells from a peer AFTER I've seen the block for that groupId as the block is the only source of KZG commitments in Gloas and you need the KGZ commitments to lookup the EL to then send your parts metadata to peers), there should NEVER be a scenario where we get cells for a column for a block before we've seen the block.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
Please let's go over this in our call this Thursday. I feel like we're not fully onbaord with the post Gloas changes 👍 

<@106441459183423488> Can we go over the Gloas changes for partial columns this week ? I am happy to put together a pre-read.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
One more thing I want us to discuss is if we need to support partial cells for Fulu or if we can just release this work post-Gloas and not deal with the complexity of having to support both the Fulu and Gloas partial column protocol in the code which will be a lot of branching.

For eg: "If slot is Fulu -> handle header otherwise discard it if it's Gloas" etc etc

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: If we can have consensus that we will release this along side/post Gloas, we can remove a fair bit of branching and complexity from the implementation rather than having to support both Fulu and Gloas Partial Columns. I am not sure if we have a strong enough reason to support Fulu Partial Columns.
